review_notes: The functions generally map well to the control AC-4(27) by focusing on redundant or independent filtering mechanisms in Kubernetes. The naming conventions are mostly followed, but a minor change was made for clarity. The functions are technically accurate and align with Kubernetes security practices.
naming_improvements: Consider using 'mandatory' instead of 'enforced' to better reflect the requirement for admission controllers to ensure network policies are applied.
title: Information Flow Enforcement - Information flow enforcement | redundant / independent filtering mechanisms when
step1_reasoning: The control AC-4(27) focuses on enforcing information flow with redundant or independent filtering mechanisms. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to ensuring that NetworkPolicies are in place to enforce traffic filtering, TLS is enforced for Ingress, mutual TLS (mTLS) is enabled in service meshes for secure communication, and admission controllers are used to enforce network policies.
